title: How to Apply for Passport Online — Fresh, Renewal, Tatkal — Complete Guide
meta_description: Passport application 2026 — online apply via Passport Seva portal, fresh application, renewal, tatkal urgent, documents, fees ₹1,500-4,000, police verification.

hero.one_liner: Apply for Indian passport online through Passport Seva portal — normal processing 30 days, Tatkal (urgent) in 1-3 days for travel emergencies

sections.heading: Understanding Passport Types & Processing
content:
FRESH PASSPORT (first-time applicant):
- For: Anyone getting a passport for the first time OR whose passport expired more than 3 years ago
- Fee: ₹1,500 (36-page booklet) or ₹2,000 (60-page booklet for frequent travelers)
- Processing: 30-45 working days AFTER police verification
- Police verification required: Yes (officer visits your address)
- Timeline breakdown: Online application → Document verification (2-5 days) → Police verification (5-15 days) → Printing (5-10 days) → Speed Post delivery (2-5 days)

PASSPORT RENEWAL:
- For: Passport expiring within 1 year OR expired less than 3 years ago
- Fee: ₹1,500 (same as fresh)
- Processing: 7-15 working days (MUCH faster than fresh)
- Police verification: May be SKIPPED if address hasn't changed (big advantage)
- Best feature: No police visit if same address — almost automatic renewal

TATKAL (URGENT):
- For: Travel emergencies — someone gets a job offer with 1-week joining deadline, or flight ticket already booked, etc.

- Fee: ₹3,500 (36-page) or ₹4,000 (60-page)
- Processing: 1-3 working days from appointment date
- Requirement: Must provide additional identity proof from authorized list (college ID from recognized institution, school certificate, employment letter from recognized employer, certificate from gazetted officer)
- No police verification typical, but biometric capture required

MINOR PASSPORT (below 18):
- Fee: ₹1,000 (valid for 5 years or until age 18, whichever comes first)
- Processing: 30 days
- Special requirement: Both parents' consent mandatory OR single parent affidavit (if one parent deceased or unknown)
- At age 18, can apply for adult passport

sections.heading: Step 1: Register & Apply Online on Passport Seva

steps.title: Visit passportindia.gov.in — Passport Seva Portal
steps.description: Go to official Ministry of External Affairs passport portal. First-time users: Click 'Register' to create account with email and password. Existing users: Click 'Login'.

steps.title: Click 'Apply for Fresh Passport' or 'Re-issue of Passport'
steps.description: Choose: (1) Fresh Passport → for first-time applicants, (2) Re-issue of Passport → for renewal or changes to existing passport. Each has its own form.

steps.title: Select Normal or Tatkal processing
steps.description: Fresh Passport: Choose Normal (30 days, ₹1,500) or Tatkal (1-3 days, ₹3,500). Renewal: Can also choose Normal or Tatkal (Tatkal renewal less common but available for urgent travel).

steps.title: Select 36-page or 60-page booklet
steps.description: 36-page: Standard, cheaper, sufficient for most people (valid 10 years, approximately 32-page capacity for visa stamps). 60-page: For frequent travelers, more visa space. Difference: ₹500 (36-page ₹1,500 vs 60-page ₹2,000).

steps.title: Fill online application form
steps.description: Very detailed form — takes 30-45 minutes. Enter: Full name (as on Aadhaar), date of birth, gender, parents' names (father + mother), present address, permanent address (if different), occupation, educational qualification, emergency contact, mother tongue, previous passport details (if any).

steps.title: Answer security questions about your address history
steps.description: The form asks: Where did you live in the last 1-3 years? Any frequent address changes trigger additional verification. Be accurate — lies here can lead to police investigation.

steps.title: Select Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) nearest to you
steps.description: Choose: Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) = dedicated passport center, OR Post Office Passport Seva Kendra (POPSK) = passport services at select post offices. Both process identically. Choose whichever is nearest to your location.

steps.title: Upload photo and signature
steps.description: Photo: Recent color photo, 2x2 inch, white background, full-face visible, JPEG format, 10KB-300KB size. CRITICAL: Photo should match specification exactly — wrong photo is the most common rejection reason. Signature: Sign on white paper, scan/photograph, upload as JPEG, 10KB-300KB. Ensure signature is clearly visible (not too light).

steps.title: Upload identity and address documents (PDF/JPEG, max 500KB each)
steps.description: Identity proof: Aadhaar card (best), Voter ID, Driving License, or PAN card — scan both sides. Address proof: Aadhaar (best), Utility bill (within 3 months), Bank statement, Passport, Voter ID. Date of birth proof: Birth certificate (for births after 26/01/1989, this is MANDATORY), 10th marksheet, Aadhaar, Passport. Scan all documents clearly — blurry scans cause rejection.

steps.title: Upload guardianship documents (if applicable)
steps.description: For minors: Birth certificate is mandatory. Parental consent letter (letter from parents confirming they consent to passport, on plain paper, signed by both). Marriage certificate (if married and name changed). Divorce decree (if previously divorced and name reverted).

steps.title: Review all information before submission
steps.description: CRITICAL STEP: Review all details carefully. Name, DOB, parents' names, address — all must match uploaded documents exactly. Mismatches cause rejections. Recheck 2-3 times before proceeding.

steps.title: Pay fee online
steps.description: Fees: Fresh 36-page: ₹1,500, Fresh 60-page: ₹2,000, Renewal 36-page: ₹1,500, Renewal 60-page: ₹2,000, Tatkal 36-page: ₹3,500, Tatkal 60-page: ₹4,000. Pay via: Net banking (all banks), Debit card, Credit card, or SBI Challan (if preferred, pay at bank and upload receipt). Online payment is instant and gives you receipt immediately.

steps.title: Download application receipt
steps.description: After payment, system generates 'Application Reference Number' (ARN) and allows download of application receipt (PDF). SAVE THIS RECEIPT — you'll need to show it at PSK appointment.

steps.title: Book appointment at Passport Seva Kendra (PSK)
steps.description: After online payment, book appointment for your nearest PSK. Calendar shows available dates and time slots. For Tatkal: book 1-3 days ahead (some PSKs allow same-day). For Normal: book 7-14 days ahead. Choose convenient date and time — appointment confirmation sent via email and SMS.

steps.title: Prepare documents for PSK visit
steps.description: Bring: (1) Application receipt (printed), (2) Original Aadhaar + color photocopy, (3) Original address proof + photocopy (utility bill/bank statement), (4) Birth certificate (original), (5) For minors: original parental consent letter, (6) For name change: marriage certificate or deed poll, (7) Appointment confirmation (printed or on mobile), (8) Any other original documents uploaded.

steps.title: Visit PSK on appointment date
steps.description: Reach 10 minutes early (appointments are strict, arriving late may cancel slot). Carry all documents. PSK process: (1) Token number issued, (2) Counter A: Document verification and cross-checking with uploaded scans, (3) Counter B: Biometric capture — photo taken at PSK (overwriting uploaded photo), fingerprints (all 10 fingers), and signature captured on tablet, (4) Counter C: Final verification and confirmation. Total time at PSK: 1-3 hours.

steps.title: No need for police interview at PSK
steps.description: Police verification happens AFTER you leave PSK (for fresh passports). You don't interact with police at PSK. Police verification is done at your home address afterward (officer visits, sometimes both times or just once).

steps.title: Police verification for fresh passport applicants
steps.description: After your PSK visit, the MEA sends your case to local police for verification. A police officer visits your address mentioned in application. They verify: (1) You actually live at that address, (2) Neighbors/landlord confirm your residence, (3) Your background (no criminal record). This takes 5-15 days. Sometimes officer calls you instead of visiting.

steps.title: If police verification fails
steps.description: Usually happens if: (1) You listed wrong address, (2) You're not present when officer visits (ask to reschedule), (3) Neighbors deny knowing you (common in large apartments). Contact your local police station to clarify. If address is correct and you're cooperative, it ALWAYS passes eventually — just may take additional time.

steps.title: Passport printing
steps.description: After police verification approval, your passport is printed. This takes 5-10 days from verification clearance.

steps.title: Delivery via Speed Post
steps.description: Printed passport is dispatched via India Post Speed Post to your registered address (the address in your application). Delivery takes 2-5 days depending on location. Track delivery at indiapost.gov.in or the tracking number provided via SMS.

steps.title: Receive passport at home
steps.description: Passport arrives in a sealed envelope. You sign receipt. Passport is now valid for: 10 years (for adults), 5 years (for minors). Check validity date inside the booklet.

sections.heading: Tatkal Passport — When & How to Use
content:
(1) Got job offer with 1-week joining date and need visa
(2) Flight ticket already booked for urgent travel
(3) Medical emergency requiring immediate international travel
(4) Family emergency (death, critical illness in family overseas)

Tatkal Requirements:
- Higher fee (₹3,500 vs ₹1,500)
- ADDITIONAL identity proof from authorized list: School/College certificate from recognized institution, OR employment letter from recognized employer, OR Certificate from Gazetted Officer (government officer)—get letter on official letterhead confirming your identity
- Fast-track appointment (book 1-3 days ahead instead of 7-14)
- Processing: PSK visit + biometrics → Police verification SAME DAY or next day → Passport printing in 1-2 days → Delivery in 1-3 days = Total 1-7 days

How to Apply Tatkal:
Process is SAME as normal fresh/renewal.

Just: (1) Select 'Tatkal' instead of 'Normal' during online application, (2) Provide additional identity proof (gazetted officer letter, school/college certificate, employment letter), (3) Pay higher fee (₹3,500), (4) Book appointment for earlier dates.

Tatkal Limitations:
- No police verification in true sense — Tatkal is based on trust and urgency
- Must provide additional strong identity proof
- Available only at major PSKs (not all centers offer Tatkal)
- Check passportindia.gov.in for which PSKs offer Tatkal in your city

sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
rows: Photo doesn't match specification (wrong size, color, background)
Rejection — application sent back, fee not refunded
Follow exact photo specs: 2x2 inch, white background, color, 10-300KB JPEG. Use professional passport photo studio (₹100-300).
Name mismatch between application and documents
Ensure name is EXACTLY same in all documents. If names differ (nickname vs full name), attach affidavit explaining.
Police verification fails or is suspicious
List the address where you've lived longest in last 1-3 years. If new to an address, update address in application to place you've lived longer.
Submitting blurry or incomplete document scans
Scan documents on good quality scanner or use phone app like CamScanner. Ensure all 4 corners of document visible, text clear.
Not providing birth certificate for adults born after 1989
If applying for first passport born after 26/01/1989, birth certificate is NON-NEGOTIABLE. Get it from municipal office first if don't have.
Applying with expired/renewed Aadhaar before update
Ensure Aadhaar is up-to-date. If recently renewed, wait for digital update before applying.
Wrong PSK selection — far from home
Choose PSK nearest to your home or workplace. Check exact location and hours before booking appointment.

sections.heading: Passport Validity & Renewal Timeline
rows: Within 1 year before expiry; or up to 3 years after expiry
At age 18: must apply for adult passport
No additional renewal needed for just name/address changes

sections.heading: Documents Needed — Complete Checklist
rows: Old passport serves as DOB proof
Required for fresh, optional for renewal
Required for fresh, optional for renewal
Required at PSK (uploaded photo is reference)
If name changed due to marriage
If name changed after previous renewal
May be skipped if address unchanged
Automatic for fresh, conditional for renewal

faqs.question: How long does passport actually take to arrive?
faqs.answer: Normal: 30-45 days total (including police verification and delivery). Tatkal: 1-7 days. Renewal: 7-15 days. After passport is dispatched by Speed Post, allow 2-5 days for postal delivery depending on your location. Track delivery on indiapost.gov.in.

faqs.question: Can I apply for passport without Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: Aadhaar is not technically mandatory, but it SIGNIFICANTLY speeds up verification. Without Aadhaar, you need multiple alternative documents (passport, voter ID, driving license) and police verification takes much longer. With Aadhaar, biometric verification is electronic — faster. Highly recommended to have Aadhaar before applying.

faqs.question: Police verification failed. What now?
faqs.answer: Usually fails due to: wrong address, not being home when officer visits, or neighbors denying your residence. Contact your local police station and explain. Request officer to visit again at a convenient time. If address was wrong, you may need to re-apply (though some PSKs allow address change). Being cooperative with police — they ALWAYS clear you eventually.

faqs.question: I have two passports (lost old one, got new one, found old). Is this legal?
faqs.answer: NO. One person = one valid passport. Having two is illegal. If you have old and new valid passports: (1) Surrendered the old one during renewal? It's not valid anymore. (2) Found your old one? Surrender it to PSK immediately. (3) Both still valid? Contact MEA and clarify — one will be cancelled.

faqs.question: Can I get a second passport for business travel (visa already in first)?
faqs.answer: YES, under specific conditions: (1) Your first passport's visa pages are nearly full (you've collected too many stamps) and won't have space for more, (2) You frequently travel and need to submit passport to embassies while possessing another passport. MEA allows 'Simultaneous Passports' in such cases. You must apply in writing with valid reason.

faqs.question: Passport for newborn baby — how to apply?
faqs.answer: Same as minor passport: Fee ₹1,000, validity 5 years (or until 18). Documents: Baby's birth certificate (MANDATORY), both parents' passports (or Aadhaar if parents don't have passports), marriage certificate of parents, newborn's photo (can be taken at PSK). Both parents must visit PSK with baby for biometric capture (baby's photo). Processing: 30 days.

faqs.question: Tatkal is urgent, but passport still takes 1-7 days. How?
faqs.answer: Because even Tatkal skips detailed police verification (trusted applicant due to strong identity proof provided). PSK + biometrics done same day, police clearance can be parallel or skipped, printing fast-tracked, delivery expedited. But system still needs 1-7 days due to printing and delivery logistics. TRUE emergency (same-day need) may need MEA's special intervention, which is rare.

disclaimer: Passport fees, processing times, and procedures as per Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) as of March 2026. MEA may update procedures, documents, or fees. Always check passportindia.gov.in for latest updates before applying.
